title: X-tra Creamy Yeasty Polish Polish Potato Dish Bread
description: A rich and creamy Polish-inspired potato bread dish featuring pistachio paste, perfect for a romantic date-night. This keto-friendly dish is cured in shio-koji and baked to perfection in a traditional Polish kazanka.
instructions: Mix the pistachio paste, pork belly, potato, butter, flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl until well combined.
Add the water and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased kazan-cauldron and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf.
Place the loaf in the preheated oven and bake for 45 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the bread is baking, mix the heavy cream and eggs in a small bowl until well combined.
Remove the bread from the oven and brush it with the cream mixture.
Return the bread to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the bread from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve warm, sliced into thick rounds.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
